description: Trigger discrimination for the Phase 7 data & retrieval cluster: retrieval/vector-store security vs corpus poisoning vs generic tenant-data design vs injection, and against the shipped multi-tenant-data-architect, tenant-isolation-reviewer, and multi-tenant-security-tester.

cases.prompt: Secure our vector search so queries only return documents the caller's tenant and role can access.
cases.reason: Retrieval-time authorization and vector-store scoping is this skill; multi-tenant-data-architect designs the general store layer and tenant-isolation-reviewer audits all surfaces, neither owns the query-filter mechanism.

cases.prompt: We ingest public web pages into our knowledge base — how do we stop attackers from poisoning what the model learns and recommends?
cases.reason: Integrity of the ingested corpus / training data is model-poisoning-reviewer; rag-security-architect owns who can retrieve what, plus poisoned-document ranking as a cross-reference.

cases.prompt: Design the multi-tenant data layer: which stores are pooled vs siloed and how tenant context propagates from request to query.
cases.reason: General per-store tenant scoping with no retrieval/vector dimension is multi-tenant-data-architect; this skill applies only when there's a retrieval/embedding surface.

cases.prompt: Write the executable cross-tenant negative test suite that proves our retrieval isolation holds, with two-tenant fixtures.
cases.reason: Authoring the executable negative suite is multi-tenant-security-tester; this skill designs the retrieval controls and seeds the cases it should encode.

cases.prompt: Stop retrieved documents from injecting instructions into our agent once they're in the prompt.
cases.reason: Untrusted-content-as-instructions once retrieved is prompt-injection-defender; rag-security-architect controls the retrieval authorization, not the in-context handling.
